capabilities.php_requirement: Lower the frozen PHP native IR through an SSA-aware adaptor with explicit blocks, operands, results, and PHI inputs.
capabilities.motivating_contract: ADR 0003 requires an explicit native IR whose control and data dependencies remain reviewable.

source_refs.evidence: The adaptor is an IR-independent function, block, instruction, and value interface.
PHI inputs expose incoming values and blocks.

capabilities.existing_behavior: IRAdaptor and PHIRef provide the required SSA-shaped boundary.

x86_64.impact: Architecture-independent adaptor used by the primary backend.

aarch64.impact: The same adaptor boundary applies to the follow-on backend.

required_tests: Compile CFGs with loop and merge PHIs through the PHP adaptor.

capabilities.php_requirement: Give every generated unit one explicit entry and model resume points without jumping into a compiled function body.
capabilities.motivating_contract: W01-C requires deterministic frame reconstruction and explicit resume targets.

source_refs.evidence: Each current function has one entry block and that block is first.

capabilities.existing_behavior: A TPDE function has one cur_entry_block; a module may contain multiple functions.

x86_64.impact: Resume code must respect single entry.

aarch64.impact: The architecture-independent restriction is identical.

required_tests: Reject a PHP lowering that names a mid-function native entry.

capabilities.php_requirement: Represent values split across multiple machine parts and register banks.
capabilities.motivating_contract: PHP frame and helper interfaces can require compound values and ABI aggregates.

source_refs.evidence: ValueParts exposes count, byte size, and register bank per part.

capabilities.existing_behavior: TPDE natively describes and assigns multipart values.

x86_64.impact: Part assignment follows x86-64 banks.

aarch64.impact: Part assignment must be tested against AArch64 banks and ABI.

required_tests: Exercise values with two parts across integer and floating-point banks.

capabilities.php_requirement: Emit helper and generated-function calls with explicit argument, result, vararg, and stack assignments.
capabilities.motivating_contract: ADR 0005 confines runtime interaction to reviewed helper boundaries.

source_refs.evidence: CCAssigner assigns arguments and returns and exposes vararg behavior.

capabilities.existing_behavior: TPDE provides calling-convention assignment and target call builders.

x86_64.impact: PHP helper ABI must bind to the x86-64 assigner.

aarch64.impact: The AArch64 helper ABI needs separate conformance tests.

required_tests: Check integer, floating, stack, return, and vararg helper-call assignments.

capabilities.php_requirement: Keep selected PHP execution context values in fixed registers where the target ABI permits it.
capabilities.motivating_contract: ADR 0004 reserves context registers but requires ordinary frame state to remain authoritative at boundaries.

source_refs.evidence: The compiler contract selects and may force fixed assignments.

capabilities.existing_behavior: Derived compilers can force an IR value into a target-selected fixed assignment.

x86_64.impact: Register selection must avoid target-reserved and EH-sensitive registers.

aarch64.impact: AArch64 has a distinct non-allocatable mask and callee-saved constraints.

required_tests: Verify fixed assignments survive calls and remain excluded from general allocation.

capabilities.php_requirement: Lower unconditional, conditional, and multi-way PHP control flow.
capabilities.motivating_contract: The frozen native IR includes explicit CFG edges and switch-like opcode control flow.

source_refs.evidence: CompilerBase exposes unconditional, conditional, and switch generation.

capabilities.existing_behavior: Architecture-independent branch helpers cover the required forms.

x86_64.impact: Primary backend branch encodings are required.

aarch64.impact: AArch64 branch reach and jump-table encodings require target tests.

required_tests: Compile dense and sparse switches plus forward and backward branches.

capabilities.php_requirement: Name generated and external entities and attach target relocations to sections.
capabilities.motivating_contract: Native images must resolve helpers and generated functions without embedding unstable process addresses.

source_refs.evidence: ELF symbols can represent undefined functions and data.
The compiler attaches relocations to the text section.

capabilities.existing_behavior: AssemblerElf and CompilerBase can create symbols and in-memory relocation records.

x86_64.impact: x86-64 relocation kinds are applied by the primary mapper.

aarch64.impact: AArch64 uses distinct CALL26, ADR, and GOT relocations.

required_tests: Resolve local, external, PC-relative, absolute, PLT, and GOT symbols.

capabilities.php_requirement: Produce inspectable ELF object bytes for diagnostics and non-live consumers.
capabilities.motivating_contract: The architecture keeps backend emission distinct from mapping and persistence.

source_refs.evidence: AssemblerElf publicly builds an ELF object byte vector.

capabilities.existing_behavior: A finalized AssemblerElf can build an object file for x86-64 or AArch64.

x86_64.impact: ELF x86-64 object generation is available.

aarch64.impact: ELF AArch64 object generation is available in TPDE but not a released PHP target.

required_tests: Parse emitted ELF headers, sections, symbols, and relocations for both TPDE targets.

capabilities.php_requirement: Place emitted sections into PHP-owned memory selected by an allocator policy.
capabilities.motivating_contract: OPcache ownership and lifecycle cannot be delegated to an anonymous one-shot mapper.

source_refs.evidence: ElfMapper directly allocates one private anonymous writable mapping.

capabilities.existing_behavior: ElfMapper owns allocation through mmap.
capabilities.missing_behavior: No allocator callback accepts section sizes, alignment, lifetime, or caller-owned storage.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Add a generic section-layout result and allocation/mapping callback; keep policy and PHP types outside TPDE.

x86_64.impact: Required for PHP-owned primary-target code memory.

aarch64.impact: The same API must allow target-specific publish operations.

required_tests: Map through a recording allocator and verify sizes, alignments, ownership, and failure cleanup.

capabilities.php_requirement: Let PHP copy sections, resolve relocations, and publish code under its own mapping lifecycle.
capabilities.motivating_contract: Request-local code and future persistent images have different ownership and publication rules.

source_refs.evidence: The only public mapper operation owns the complete mapping procedure.

capabilities.existing_behavior: ElfMapper offers a fixed map operation and address lookup.
capabilities.missing_behavior: Section layout, copy, relocation application, protection, and registration are not separable public stages.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Expose a target-neutral finalized image view and relocation application entry point usable by a caller-owned mapper.

x86_64.impact: Needed to integrate primary code with PHP memory ownership.

aarch64.impact: Must include target publish and branch-range responsibilities.

required_tests: Compare ElfMapper and a test custom mapper over the same finalized image.

capabilities.php_requirement: Serialize relocation records without resolved process addresses and replay them in each process.
capabilities.motivating_contract: A relocatable OPcache image must survive process and address-space changes.

source_refs.evidence: Relocation access exists but is private and available only to the friend mapper.

capabilities.existing_behavior: Assembler sections retain relocation records until final mapping or object generation.
capabilities.missing_behavior: There is no stable public relocation/symbol image view designed for persistence and replay.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Expose immutable section, symbol, and relocation descriptors with target-defined relocation kinds and no live pointers.

x86_64.impact: Primary persistent records need x86-64 relocation semantics.

aarch64.impact: AArch64 records additionally need range-safe call and GOT handling.

required_tests: Serialize, reload at a different base, resolve process symbols, and compare relocated bytes.

capabilities.php_requirement: Resolve helper addresses independently in every process after loading an address-neutral image.
capabilities.motivating_contract: ASLR and per-process runtime state prohibit persisting helper pointers.

source_refs.evidence: Mapping accepts a resolver and stores resolved symbol addresses in mapper-local state.

capabilities.existing_behavior: ElfMapper invokes a caller resolver and stores symbol addresses per mapper instance.

x86_64.impact: Resolver supplies helper and PLT/GOT targets per process.

aarch64.impact: The same lifecycle applies with AArch64 relocation kinds.

required_tests: Map identical input with two resolvers and prove addresses are not shared or serialized.

capabilities.php_requirement: Never leave writable executable code pages; publish written sections with final permissions.
capabilities.motivating_contract: ADR 0008 requires explicit code-memory hardening and auditable transitions.

source_refs.evidence: The mapper starts writable and applies final per-section read/write/execute permissions.

capabilities.existing_behavior: ElfMapper maps RW, copies and relocates, then derives final protections from ELF section flags.
capabilities.missing_behavior: The final mprotect logic independently accepts SHF_WRITE and SHF_EXECINSTR, so it does not reject a writable-executable input section; a generic custom-mapper publication contract is also absent.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Reject any writable-executable allocated section and expose a checked target publication step for caller-owned mappings.

x86_64.impact: Primary mapper must retain the RW-to-RX transition.

aarch64.impact: The transition must be ordered with instruction-cache publication.

required_tests: Inspect mappings after publication and assert no writable executable range.

capabilities.php_requirement: Perform the architecture-required data/instruction cache maintenance before executing newly written AArch64 code.
capabilities.motivating_contract: The follow-on target must not execute stale instructions after mapping or patching.

source_refs.evidence: The complete copy-to-publish sequence applies relocations and protections but contains no instruction-cache maintenance call.

capabilities.existing_behavior: ElfMapper copies bytes and changes protections.
capabilities.missing_behavior: The reviewed publish path has no AArch64 instruction-cache flush or generic publish hook.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Add target-aware cache maintenance before executable publication and expose the same obligation to custom mappers.

x86_64.impact: x86-64 instruction caches are coherent for this publication model.

aarch64.impact: Release is prohibited until cache maintenance is implemented and tested.

required_tests: Generate, publish, and repeatedly execute patched AArch64 code on real hardware or a cache-faithful runner.

capabilities.php_requirement: Guarantee that every AArch64 direct-call relocation reaches its target or a range-safe veneer.
capabilities.motivating_contract: Custom and persistent mappings may place helper targets outside CALL26 range.

source_refs.evidence: CALL26 falls back to a PLT slot, but the API does not expose a general layout/range guarantee for caller-owned mappings.

capabilities.existing_behavior: ElfMapper creates in-mapping PLT slots and redirects an initially out-of-range external call to one.
capabilities.missing_behavior: After PLT fallback the implementation checks a 32-bit signed displacement rather than CALL26's aligned signed 28-bit byte range, so even ElfMapper does not prove the replacement is encodable. No reusable veneer/layout contract exists for custom mappers.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Correct the post-fallback CALL26 range check and provide target layout constraints plus a veneer allocator used by both ElfMapper and custom relocation application.

x86_64.impact: The AArch64 CALL26 range rule does not apply.

aarch64.impact: Required before AArch64 can be released.

required_tests: Relocate calls just inside and outside CALL26 range and force multiple veneer regions.

capabilities.php_requirement: Emit unwind metadata and register live mappings with the process unwinder.
capabilities.motivating_contract: Native frames must remain diagnosable and safely unwindable across approved helper boundaries.

source_refs.evidence: FunctionWriter emits CIE/FDE-oriented EH-frame records.
ElfMapper registers the mapped EH-frame data.

capabilities.existing_behavior: TPDE emits EH-frame data; ElfMapper registers it and deregisters it on reset.

x86_64.impact: Primary live mappings require registration and teardown.

aarch64.impact: AArch64 CFI and registration need separate validation.

required_tests: Walk a native stack through generated code and verify deregistration before unmap.

capabilities.php_requirement: Capture the exact machine-code offset associated with each semantic statepoint.
capabilities.motivating_contract: W01-C keys safepoint, bailout, and resume metadata by a stable native offset.

source_refs.evidence: FunctionWriter exposes the current section offset, but not a semantic statepoint callback.

capabilities.existing_behavior: A compiler implementation can query the current writer offset.
capabilities.missing_behavior: TPDE has no generic before/after instruction statepoint hook that associates an IR marker with the finalized offset.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Add a generic derived-compiler callback carrying an opaque adaptor statepoint reference, phase, section, and offset.

x86_64.impact: Offsets index primary-target bailout metadata.

aarch64.impact: Offsets have the same meaning but different instruction alignment.

required_tests: Check before/after hook offsets around zero-byte, fixed-size, and call-emitting instructions.

capabilities.php_requirement: At selected statepoints force every requested live value into an explicitly recoverable location.
capabilities.motivating_contract: W01-C forbids bailout metadata that names values unavailable to frame reconstruction.

source_refs.evidence: Forced branch spilling exists, but skips fixed assignments and liveness-dead values and is tied to block transitions.

capabilities.existing_behavior: Individual assignment parts can spill, and branch handling can force its own spill policy.
capabilities.missing_behavior: There is no statepoint API to materialize an explicit live-value set while preserving a queryable location record, including fixed values.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Add a generic materialize(values, policy) operation that returns failure rather than silently omitting an unrecoverable part.

x86_64.impact: Required at every primary bailout-capable statepoint.

aarch64.impact: Must handle target-specific fixed and floating-point registers.

required_tests: Materialize dirty, clean, fixed, multipart, stack, register, and variable-reference values.

capabilities.php_requirement: Export an immutable snapshot of each live value part's register, stack, constant, or unavailable location.
capabilities.motivating_contract: W01-C frame reconstruction consumes explicit value locations rather than compiler internals.

source_refs.evidence: AssignmentPartRef can inspect register validity, stack validity, size, and frame offset.
CompilerBase exposes an assignment only by internal local index, not as a stable snapshot API.

capabilities.existing_behavior: Mutable assignment state contains enough raw facts for many register and stack locations during compilation.
capabilities.missing_behavior: There is no public immutable enumeration, no constant/unavailable vocabulary, and no lifetime boundary for exported locations.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Add a generic callback visitor over value parts that copies normalized location descriptors at a statepoint.

x86_64.impact: Descriptors must name x86-64 register banks and CFA-relative stack locations.

aarch64.impact: Register identifiers and stack rules require an AArch64 mapping.

required_tests: Snapshot multipart values before and after spills, calls, and fixed assignment changes.

capabilities.php_requirement: Combine code offsets and normalized locations with PHP frame reconstruction metadata in a versioned stackmap table.
capabilities.motivating_contract: W01-C defines the consumer-facing safepoint and bailout record independently of backend allocation state.

source_refs.evidence: TPDE assignment state has frame offsets and parts but no PHP frame or stackmap schema.

capabilities.existing_behavior: TPDE tracks backend frame offsets and assignments while compiling.
capabilities.missing_behavior: TPDE intentionally has no PHP VM-slot, opline, resume, or bailout table format.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Build the versioned frame/stackmap records in PHP from generic offset and location callbacks.

x86_64.impact: PHP consumes x86-64 normalized locations.

aarch64.impact: The PHP schema stays stable while location decoding becomes target-specific.

required_tests: Round-trip PHP frame maps and reject missing, duplicate, or non-materialized values.

capabilities.php_requirement: Resume or enter optimized code only through explicit functions that reconstruct the required native context.
capabilities.motivating_contract: W01-C requires resume identities and frame transitions to be explicit and independently validatable.

source_refs.evidence: A module can expose multiple separately compiled functions.
Each such function retains one entry block.

capabilities.existing_behavior: TPDE compiles multiple single-entry functions and assigns symbols to them.
capabilities.missing_behavior: TPDE has no PHP resume-ID dispatcher or VM-frame reconstruction policy, and mid-function native entries are not modeled.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Generate a separate single-entry resume/OSR function per accepted shape and dispatch to it from PHP-owned metadata.

x86_64.impact: Primary resume stubs use the x86-64 PHP ABI.

aarch64.impact: Separate AArch64 stubs need ABI and context-register validation.

required_tests: Prove all resume IDs target function symbols and no metadata points inside a function body.

capabilities.php_requirement: Publish generated ranges and names to PHP diagnostics, perf tooling, and crash reports.
capabilities.motivating_contract: ADR 0007 requires observability for native compilation and execution.

source_refs.evidence: AssemblerElf retains symbol definitions, names, sections, offsets, and sizes.
ElfMapper exposes mapped symbol addresses and the mapped range.

capabilities.existing_behavior: TPDE provides symbols, ELF object data, and live mapped ranges.
capabilities.missing_behavior: It does not publish PHP naming, perf-map/jitdump events, or lifecycle telemetry.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Translate TPDE symbols and ranges into PHP-owned diagnostic and perf events.

x86_64.impact: Primary generated code must be symbolized.

aarch64.impact: The same event schema applies with AArch64 machine code.

required_tests: Check symbol/range publication and removal on code lifecycle transitions.

capabilities.php_requirement: Compile concurrently without sharing mutable adaptor, compiler, assembler, or mapper state between threads.
capabilities.motivating_contract: PHP builds and SAPIs may compile from multiple threads even if a request executes on one thread.

source_refs.evidence: CompilerBase is a mutable compiler object coupled to an adaptor instance.
ElfMapper owns mutable mapping state and is non-copyable.

capabilities.existing_behavior: Compilation and mapping state is instance-owned; no reviewed source establishes safe concurrent use of one instance.
capabilities.missing_behavior: TPDE does not state or enforce a shared-instance thread-safety contract.
capabilities.minimal_api_or_shim: Use one adaptor/compiler/assembler/mapper graph per compilation and synchronize only PHP cache publication.

x86_64.impact: Per-compilation ownership is mandatory on the primary target.

aarch64.impact: The same ownership rule applies.

required_tests: Run independent compiler instances concurrently under a race detector when available.

capabilities.php_requirement: Reuse compiler objects only after all per-compilation state has been reset deterministically.
capabilities.motivating_contract: Request isolation forbids symbols, assignments, or layout state leaking between compilations.

source_refs.evidence: Reset clears adaptor, stack lists, assembler, function symbols, labels, and personality symbols.

capabilities.existing_behavior: CompilerBase documents reset-before-recompile and clears its reusable state.

x86_64.impact: Primary compiler reuse must invoke reset.

aarch64.impact: Target-specific reset state needs the same regression coverage.

required_tests: Compile two disjoint modules with reset and detect any cross-module symbol or byte leakage.

capabilities.php_requirement: Build the integration with C++20 while TPDE itself uses no C++ exception handling.
capabilities.motivating_contract: The native engine build boundary must be explicit and compatible with PHP failure handling.

source_refs.evidence: TPDE requires C++20.
Exception handling defaults off and non-MSVC C++ compilation uses -fno-exceptions.

capabilities.existing_behavior: The upstream build requires C++20 and defaults to no C++ exceptions.

x86_64.impact: Required build mode for the primary backend.

aarch64.impact: The same language and exception mode applies.

required_tests: Configure and compile TPDE integration with C++20 and exceptions disabled.

capabilities.php_requirement: Provide Linux/ELF x86-64 code generation as the first released native target.
capabilities.motivating_contract: The W01 execution frame names Linux/ELF x86-64 as the primary platform.

source_refs.evidence: TPDE states that its ELF x86-64 backend is supported.
TPDE provides an x86-64 ELF assembler.

capabilities.existing_behavior: TPDE has an x86-64 compiler and ELF assembler/mapper relocation path.

x86_64.impact: This is the release target.

aarch64.impact: This capability is x86-64-specific.

required_tests: Run the complete native contract and backend suite on Linux/ELF x86-64.

capabilities.php_requirement: Keep AArch64 design-compatible without declaring it released before target-specific gaps close.
capabilities.motivating_contract: W01 treats AArch64 as analyzed and unreleased follow-on scope.

source_refs.evidence: TPDE states that its ELF AArch64 backend is supported.
TPDE provides an AArch64 ELF assembler.

capabilities.existing_behavior: TPDE has an AArch64 compiler and ELF assembler/mapper relocation path.

x86_64.impact: This capability is AArch64-specific.

aarch64.impact: Backend existence does not imply PHP release; cache, veneer, ABI, and contract tests remain gates.

required_tests: Compile target corpus and run target-specific relocation, ABI, unwind, cache, and veneer suites before release.
